summary: The W205 C63's M177 twin-turbo V8 is a powerful and generally reliable engine. Maintenance costs are high by nature, and some components are not designed for the abuse enthusiasts deliver.

issues.name: Head Bolt Failure (Early M177)
issues.description: Early 2015–2016 M177 engines had a head bolt issue that could cause coolant intrusion into the cylinders. Mercedes addressed this with revised bolts.

issues.name: Engine Mount Failure
issues.description: The factory hydraulic engine mounts can fail, causing excessive engine movement, vibration, and clunking under acceleration.

issues.name: Air Suspension Compressor Failure
issues.description: If equipped with AIRMATIC air suspension, the compressor and air struts can fail, causing the car to sag or ride unevenly.

issues.name: Differential Bushing Wear
issues.description: The rear differential bushings wear under hard use, causing clunking during gear changes and a vague rear end feel.

issues.name: Rear Subframe Bushing Noise
issues.description: The rear subframe bushings can develop a creaking or groaning noise over bumps. Common on enthusiastically driven C63s.

buyingTips: 2017+ models are preferred — they addressed the head bolt issue and received other refinements
Verify the head bolt recall/TSB has been completed on 2015–2016 models — this is critical
Air suspension-equipped cars have higher maintenance costs — coil spring cars are simpler and cheaper to maintain
The M177 engine is hand-built and generally robust — proper oil changes with AMG-spec 0W-40 every 10,000 miles are key
Check for aftermarket tunes — the M177 responds very well to tuning and many C63s have been modified
